flows.browserProcessing: The page calculation engine processes inputs in the browser unless the tool explicitly labels a server-backed lookup or AI action.
storageLocations: localStorage only when a save/draft control is used
flows.retention: Unsaved values last for the page session; saved local values remain until the user clears them or browser storage.
flows.accountAssociation: None for guest calculation; a separate sync action can associate a supported saved item with an account.
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The calculator label identifies any separate network-backed action before it is used.
flows.deletionBehavior: Clear/reset removes the current values; saved local values can be removed from the relevant history or browser storage.
flows.exportBehavior: Supported results can be copied or downloaded locally; export behavior is tool-specific.
pointOfUseSurfaces: public calculator input and result areas
exceptions: AI, TTS, email, account sync, payment, vault uploads, analytics, and live lookups have separate flows below.

dataEntered: the fields or document excerpts listed in the AI disclosure
flows.browserProcessing: The browser assembles a bounded request after the user accepts the AI disclosure.
networkDestinations: configured Anthropic API endpoint when enabled
storageLocations: provider/service logs subject to deployed configuration
flows.retention: AfroTools does not promise zero retention; deployed service and provider policies control operational logs.
flows.accountAssociation: Only when the calling feature deliberately includes authenticated context; the consent disclosure must state this.
thirdPartyProcessors: Anthropic when the configured provider is available
flows.consentOrDisclosure: Explicit point-of-use consent lists what is sent, why it is sent, and the local-only alternative.
flows.deletionBehavior: Local input can be cleared immediately; provider/service log deletion follows the applicable service process.
flows.exportBehavior: AI output can be copied or exported only through the calling tool's labelled controls.
exceptions: Provider availability depends on deployed configuration and a successful service request.

flows.browserProcessing: The browser keeps a local copy and submits a supported item to the workspace API after sign-in.
flows.retention: Local data remains until cleared; account data remains until the user deletes it or the account/service retention process applies.
flows.consentOrDisclosure: Sign-in and the sync/save control disclose that selected supported items are sent to the account workspace.
flows.deletionBehavior: Supported synced items can be deleted through the workspace API; local copies must be removed separately.
flows.exportBehavior: Workspace data can be exported where the relevant history/tool exposes export; local JSON export remains workflow-specific.
exceptions: Unsupported item types and failed requests remain local; sync is not a universal multi-device guarantee.

dataEntered: payment details entered on the provider surface
flows.browserProcessing: AfroTools initializes checkout and records pending/status metadata; card or bank details are entered on the configured provider flow.
storageLocations: limited pending checkout state in the browser
flows.retention: Provider and transaction-record retention follows the configured provider and applicable accounting requirements.
flows.accountAssociation: Yes, billing and entitlement status are associated with the purchasing account.
thirdPartyProcessors: Supabase when subscription state is stored there
flows.consentOrDisclosure: Checkout identifies the provider and requires an explicit purchase action.
flows.deletionBehavior: Payment records may be retained for legal/accounting reasons; account and subscription controls handle eligible profile/status deletion.
flows.exportBehavior: Provider receipts and account billing records are available according to the active provider integration.
exceptions: Accepted payment methods, refunds, renewal, and cancellation paths depend on the active provider configuration.

dataEntered: browser/request metadata exposed by network delivery
flows.browserProcessing: Analytics scripts and product events must wait for analytics consent and exclude raw sensitive workflow content.
networkDestinations: configured Google Analytics endpoint after consent
configured Microsoft Clarity endpoint after consent if enabled
flows.retention: Browser consent persists until changed; provider retention follows the configured analytics property.
flows.accountAssociation: Product analytics is not intended to include raw account or sensitive input content; provider identifiers/request metadata may still exist.
thirdPartyProcessors: Google Analytics when configured and consented
Microsoft Clarity when configured and consented
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The cookie/analytics control must precede analytics script loading and offer accept/reject choices.
flows.deletionBehavior: Users can withdraw consent for future collection; provider-held data follows provider deletion/configuration controls.
flows.exportBehavior: No user-facing raw analytics export is promised by the product.
exceptions: Network request metadata and provider-side retention cannot be described as fully anonymous.

flows.browserProcessing: Named local PDF/image operations read and transform the selected content in browser memory.
storageLocations: local browser drafts only when the user saves them
flows.retention: In-memory content ends with the page/session; saved local drafts remain until removed.
flows.accountAssociation: None unless the user separately chooses account sync or vault upload.
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The local operation is labelled local; network-backed document actions are separate controls.
flows.deletionBehavior: Closing/resetting clears in-memory content; saved local drafts can be removed by the user.
flows.exportBehavior: Generated files are downloaded locally through the browser.
exceptions: TTS, AI, email, sync, and vault controls use separate network flows.

dataEntered: the selected document text/file or derived excerpt required by the chosen network action
flows.browserProcessing: The browser prepares the selected content after an explicit TTS, AI, email, sync, or delivery action.
networkDestinations: the configured downstream processor for that action
storageLocations: service/provider logs or delivery records as disclosed
flows.retention: Depends on the selected function and provider; no universal never-stored promise is made.
flows.accountAssociation: Only when the selected action requires an authenticated account or includes account context.
thirdPartyProcessors: the provider named at the point of use
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The action must identify what content leaves the device and why before submission.
flows.deletionBehavior: Local content can be cleared immediately; remote deletion follows the named service/provider process.
flows.exportBehavior: The selected service can return audio, document output, delivery status, or other labelled output.
exceptions: Each network document feature must provide its own narrower processor and retention disclosure.

flows.browserProcessing: The browser validates the selected file and explicitly uploads it after the signed-in user chooses the vault action.
networkDestinations: AfroTools Supabase storage/project zpclagtgczsygrgztlts when the canonical client is active
flows.retention: Uploaded files and metadata remain until deletion or the account/service retention process applies.
flows.accountAssociation: Yes, vault objects and metadata are associated with the signed-in user.
flows.consentOrDisclosure: Upload requires sign-in and an explicit vault upload action.
flows.deletionBehavior: The vault delete control removes the object and associated metadata when the service succeeds.
flows.exportBehavior: Users can download their vault file where the vault control is available.
exceptions: The legacy fallback client contains a non-canonical project URL and must not support an unconditional availability promise.

dataEntered: contact details and message fields shown by the form
flows.browserProcessing: The browser validates fields and submits them only after the user activates the form action.
flows.retention: Depends on the disclosed form purpose and configured delivery/store process.
flows.accountAssociation: Only when the form is part of an authenticated account flow or the user supplies an account email.
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The form states the submission purpose and requires an explicit submit action.
flows.deletionBehavior: Unsubscribe and deletion requests are processed through the applicable email, account, or privacy workflow; immediate completion is not guaranteed.
flows.exportBehavior: No automatic user-facing export is promised unless the specific form provides one.
exceptions: Delivery and provider retention may be controlled by the configured third-party service.

dataEntered: lookup parameters such as country, symbol, route, or requested dataset
flows.browserProcessing: The browser sends the bounded lookup request and renders source, observed timestamp, freshness state, and failure state from the response contract.
networkDestinations: named AfroTools data function or endpoint
named upstream source when the service calls one
flows.retention: Depends on the named endpoint/cache; user-entered sensitive content must not be included unless separately disclosed.
flows.accountAssociation: Normally none; authenticated endpoints must state when account context is used.
thirdPartyProcessors: the upstream data provider named by the feature
flows.consentOrDisclosure: The result surface shows the source, observation timestamp, freshness state, and an unavailable/stale fallback.
flows.deletionBehavior: Ephemeral lookup parameters are not exposed as user-stored records; provider/service logs follow the endpoint policy.
flows.exportBehavior: Exports must preserve source and freshness context when the external data affects the result.
exceptions: Static fixtures and cached data cannot be labelled live; request success alone is insufficient without source and timestamp.
